title: Guidance for populating GenomeTrakr metadata templates (BioSample and SRA)

before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"fcab8","text":"Before collecting sequence data for your isolates, ensure that you can provide the minimum metadata recommended by your coordinating surveilliance body. The INSDC, in collaboration with the Global Microbial Identifer (GMI) (https://www.globalmicrobialidentifier.org), recommends using the Pathogen metadata template for pathogen surveilliance submissions: (NCBI: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pathogens/submit-data/and EMBL-EBI: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ena/submit/pathogen-data). materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"6uvc2","text":"Gather the following contextual information for each pure culture isolate:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":74}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9vk71","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1pt5g","text":"organism name","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"737bf","text":"lab name that collected the sample","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b4vkf","text":"collection date","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cmclm","text":"collection source","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2fcl5","text":"Geographic location of sample collection","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f67cf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Guidance for organizing and populating the metadata templates required for direct submission to NCBI. This guidance is applicable for most enterics and/or microbial pathogens. **** If your laboratory uses the BioNumerics platform for submission, please follow this protocol . **** Two metadata templates are required: 1. BioSample metadata (metadata describing the sample source and submitter) 2. SRA metadata (metadata describing the sequence data collection)
Template for BioSample submission: Download the GenomeTrakr-extended pathogen package and follow the guidance included in this template. GT-pathogen package-OHE v0.2.3.xlsx

DRAFT One Health Enteric Package, announced at IAFP 2021 (and the fall GenomeTrakr meeting) is ready for review and comment. NOTE: DO NOT USE THIS VERSION FOR NCBI SUBMISSION. We expect the v1.0 release to be ready for use in Fall 2021. Please download the file, review the attributes, and provide feedback here or email directly to ruth.timme@fda.hhs.gov. One Health Enteric Package-DRAFT_v0.5.1-Nov1.xlsx NOTE: DO NOT USE THIS VERSION FOR NCBI SUBMISSION. We expect the v1.0 release to be ready for use in Fall 2021.

Template for SRA metadata submission: Download the " Metadata spreadsheet with sample names " file from the NCBI Submission Templates page: https://submit.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/templates/ And follow the guidance in the following table: PRO TIPS: If you have sequences to submit that belong to more than one BioProject, create a separate submission + metadata table for each of your BioProjects. Entering fastq filenames in the spreadsheet : On a Mac, you can directly copy the file names from the folder into a spreadsheet. This is not possible on a PC using copy and paste but can be done with some command-line operation. Finally, it is important to develop a QA/QC step to make sure the files are associated with the correct sample name. For example, use a left function in excel to strip of the appended text in the file name and then use the exact match to make sure the name matches the sample name.
%s %s Save the second sheet (SRA_data) as a TSV (tab-delimited file) for upload in the “SRA metadata” tab within the submission portal. *NCBI should also accept the original excel formatted file.
creator: Maria Balkey
